text: The surgeon injects vasopressin into the appropriate plane around a fifteen-centimeter intramural myoma. Approximately two hundred ml of vasopressin is used, diluted to five international units in twenty ml saline. Using a harmonic scalpel, the seromuscular layer is cut transversely due to the ipsilateral port position.

The surgeon observes the effects of vasopressin mixed with diluted saline, which creates transient ischemia and facilitates hydro dissection, forming a clear plane around the myoma. The myoma screw is used to fix the myoma, aiding in its enucleation. The surgeon carefully separates the muscle layers, ensuring no residual muscle is left behind.

The surgeon uses a 10mm myoma screw to provide anterior traction on the large myoma occupying the pelvis. With the assistance of a harmonic device or suction irrigation instrument, the myoma is carefully enucleated from the surrounding uterine tissue. The fallopian tube and ovary are visually confirmed to be intact and unaffected by the procedure.

The surgeon continues to separate the myoma from the uterus, with approximately seventy-five percent of the myoma now detached. The remaining part of the uterus is visible below the myoma. The myoma screw is repositioned multiple times to optimize traction during the dissection.

The surgeon uses a myoma screw to provide traction while carefully preserving the muscle plane during the separation of a deep intramural myoma. The base of the myoma is exposed, and the surgeon cautiously separates any feeding vessels. The majority of the myoma is now separated, with attention given to identifying and removing any small, hidden sub-centimeter myomas.

The myoma has been removed, and the surgeon begins suturing the uterine wall using a number one Vicryl suture. The procedure involves two-layer suturing, starting with the internal layer. The surgeon uses an endoskeletal needle for intracorporeal continuous suturing, beginning from the right side, moving to the left, and returning to the right.

The surgeon uses a number one Vicryl suture for continuous suturing of the uterine muscle layers, carefully handling the needle with various techniques to ensure precise placement. The needle is manipulated by brushing at the junction of two-thirds and one-third with the upper jaw, transferring from left to right, and hanging like a pendulum before crossing to the right.

The surgeon begins suturing the uterine incision following the removal of the myoma, using a laparoscopic approach. The suturing progresses from right to left, emphasizing the importance of precise technique to prevent uterine rupture in future pregnancies.

The surgeon performs ipsilateral suturing with a perpendicular needle orientation, using a Maryland instrument to approximate the muscle layer. The suturing technique is applied to a transverse incision, and the muscle layer is gradually approximated, reducing any minor bleeding observed.

The surgeon discusses the different grades of myoma, highlighting that grade zero myomas are predominantly pedunculated and can be directly excised without suturing, while deep-situated myomas require proper suturing after removal.
The surgeon completes the internal layer of suturing on the healthy serosa using a continuous technique, preparing to return for the second layer of suturing.

The surgeon uses the suture as traction to approximate the outer layer of the uterus during a laparoscopic myomectomy. The needle holder's open jaw is used to tighten the suture, while the left hand instrument lifts the suture. The needle is pressed upward by the upper jaw to continue taking bites, ensuring the serosal layer is approximated. This technique helps restore the uterus's shape, obliterate dead space, and create smooth margins to prevent adhesions. The vasopressin effect, which aids in reducing bleeding, lasts for about twenty minutes.

So you need to have the patience to do the suturing. And your patience can be rewarded by a good result and good outcome of the pregnancy. And slowly it is getting sutured. Once the patient will have the post-operatively, the size of the uterus Again we will modify and it will become completely normal. Right now it looks like bulky, but once it will be re-modified, it will become completely normal in the shape. Although vagina delivery is possible post-operatively after myomectomy, but preferably patients should be ready for caesarean section. and at the thirty-seventh or thirty-eighth week of the pregnancy, she should be in continuous contact with the gynaecologist for the purpose. And if it is required, caesarean section has to be considered the first choice. Laparoscopic myomectomy or even open, especially intramural myoma, has this problem of the weakening of the muscles. Patients should conceive at least after six months so that uterus should be given at least six months rest so that it will heal properly.

Throughout the laparoscopic myomectomy, the surgeon performs continuous suturing without using suction irrigation, resulting in minimal blood loss. The remaining blood is visible in the surgical field and will be removed at the end. The surgeon takes deep bites into healthy serosa, supporting the suture with the left hand, and uses both intracorporeal and extracorporeal knots to secure the sutures. The tail of the suture is pulled to tighten the inner layer before termination.

The surgeon performs a laparoscopic myomectomy, using a C-shaped technique to create a double wrap and tie a surgeon's knot. The knot is secured with alternating wraps, and the suture is cut at the end, completing the suturing process.

The surgeon completes the suturing process and carefully removes the suture. Suction and irrigation are performed to wash out collected blood, ensuring a clean surgical field. The surgeon then begins the morselation of the myoma, a process that requires careful handling to avoid damaging surrounding tissues. The morselator tip remains in a neutral area while the myoma tissue is gradually morselated.
So this was a simple case of laparoscopic myomectomy. Thank you very much for watching this video. Have a nice day.
